The human behind it

Passion for the craft. Built to show it.

I'm Korey. I'm a chef — I came up cooking West Indian food, not sushi. But I fell hard for Japanese cuisine and the culture around it: the discipline, the restraint, the reverence for a single perfect piece of fish.

And I kept watching dishes that deserved the world get photographed in ways that flattened them. Beautiful food, killed by flat light and a phone held at the wrong angle. It bothered me more than it should have.

So I built KOKUWABI. A chef's eye for food is a chef's eye — I know what a plate looks like at its peak, the angle that catches the sear, the timing of a pour, the second a piece of toro looks alive. I've spent serious time studying how the best Japanese food is plated and lit, and I point that eye at every clip we make.

The tools move fast. The eye belongs to a chef who reveres your craft.

— Korey Bogle, Chef & Founder
korey@kokuwabi.com

Before you ask

The honest answers.

無料

See it before you pay.

Your first sample clip is free. We'll make something for your restaurant — if it doesn't stop the scroll, you owe us nothing.

Why not just use AI myself?

The tools are easy to get. Knowing which eight seconds out of a hundred actually make someone book a table — that's the job. You're hiring the eye and the taste, not the software.

Is this real video of my food?

Straight answer: we start from your own dishes — your photos and your plating — and bring them into cinematic motion using an advanced rendering pipeline that preserves the exact texture of your glaze, the sear on your wagyu, the grain of your rice. It's your real food, amplified — delivered in days instead of weeks. We always match it to how you actually serve it, and if it ever doesn't look like your restaurant, we fix it until it does.

How fast do I get it?

72 hours from brief to delivery on most projects. Traditional agencies take six to eight weeks. We move at the speed your feed actually needs.

What if I don't like it?

The first sample is free, so you risk nothing to start. On paid projects, we revise until it's right — we'd rather you be thrilled than finished.

Do I need a big following already?

No. Great content is what grows the following — not the other way around. The work performs whether you have 200 followers or 200,000.

一期一会 One meeting, one chance

Let's make your food famous.

Takes 20 seconds. You'll pick a time on the next screen.
